text: Fill in the blanks about the hip joint anatomy.
questions: The hip joint is a *ball-and-socket/ball and socket/spheroidal* type of synovial joint, designed for *stability/stability:unlike the shoulder* over mobility.
The acetabulum is formed by three bones: the *ilium/ilium:superior* (superior), *ischium/ischium:posteroinferior* (posteroinferior), and *pubis/pubis:anteroinferior* (anteroinferior).
The strongest ligament in the body is the *iliofemoral/iliofemoral ligament* ligament, also known as the Y-ligament of *Bigelow*. It resists *extension/hyperextension* and lateral rotation.
Fractures of the femoral neck risk *avascular necrosis/AVN* of the femoral head because the *retinacular/retinacular:vessels along the neck* blood vessels are damaged, cutting off blood supply.
In posterior dislocation of the hip (common in RTAs), the limb is held in *flexion*, *adduction*, and *internal rotation/medial rotation*.
